title: Agent Eval Scenario
description: Schema for a single eval scenario that tests the Agent via multi-turn interaction with a simulated human agent.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for IDE autocomplete.

id.description: Unique scenario identifier (kebab-case). Used in session names, results, and filtering.

prompt.description: The initial prompt sent to the Agent (turn 1). This is what the simulated human says first.

description.description: Scenario goal — what the simulated human is trying to accomplish. Guides the scenario agent's multi-turn behavior.

agent.description: The agent-cli agent name to test against. Defaults to the agent agent.

targets.description: Which runtime formats this scenario is compatible with. 'power' = Agent (POWER.md + steering/), 'plugin' = Agent Plugin (SKILL.md + references/). Empty or omitted means compatible with both.

max_turns.description: Maximum turns for the scenario agent. Journey scenarios need 15-20, step-gate tests need 5-8.

simulated_human_guidance.description: Extra behavioral instructions for the scenario agent (e.g., 'Choose .NET when asked about scope'). Optional.

workspace_setup.description: Description of required workspace state for this scenario (e.g., 'Workspace with .NET .csproj files'). For documentation only — not enforced.

workspace_dir.description: Path to a directory of fixture files to copy into the workspace before the scenario starts. Resolved relative to the scenario JSON file. Copied recursively — directory structure is preserved.

mocks.description: Mock definitions for CLI commands and MCP tools. When provided, the eval framework intercepts matching calls and returns canned responses instead of hitting real services.

cli.description: Canned responses for CLI commands (e.g., 'my-cli list'). Matched by prefix.

command.description: Command prefix to match (e.g., 'my-cli list --json'). Matches if the actual command starts with this string.

mcp.description: Canned responses for MCP tool calls. The mock MCP server replaces the real MCP server.

response.description: Response payload to return. Will be JSON-serialized as the tool result.

assertions.description: Assertions to check against the interaction transcript.

name.description: Unique assertion name (snake_case). Used in grade output.

type.description: Assertion type. Deterministic types (contains, tool_called) are checked first; llm_judge uses LLM reasoning.

description.description: What this assertion checks. Included in grade output for context.

check.description: What to check. String for most types. Array of strings for transcript_contains_any. For llm_judge, this is the evaluation criteria/question.
